statement.p: In which of the following situations would the formula \displaystyle{P}=\frac{{{A}{\left(\frac{{r}}{{n}}\right)}}}{{{\left({1}+\frac{{r}}{{n}}\right)}^{{{n}{t}}}-{1}}} be best used?

ol.li: You want to know the future value of an account in \displaystyle{10} years which was started with a one-time deposit of \displaystyle\${5000}.
You want to know how much would be in an annuity after \displaystyle{5} years with regular monthly deposits of \displaystyle\${250}.
You want to determine the amount of a one-time deposit into an account with \displaystyle{6}\% interest compounded weekly.
You want to determine the amount to regularly deposit into an annuity so that there is \displaystyle\${10},{000} in the account in \displaystyle{10} years.

ul.li: D: You want to determine the amount to regularly deposit into an annuity so that there is \displaystyle\${10},{000} in the account in \displaystyle{10} years.
